title: A Curated Top 10 List - Produced by Tom Cohen
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=H-jiaEptrx0

Daniel (@dungeondive) and patron Tom Cohen craft a unique "desert island" collection—not Daniel's personal top 10 favorites, but rather a curated selection across ten distinct categories that every serious adventure game collection should include. Starting with something unique, Daniel selects Hand of Fate: Ordeals, a deck-building adventure game that feels genuinely like an adventure despite its card-driven mechanics. For old-school vibes, Dungeon Crusade offers that Warhammer Quest energy with solo-friendly mechanics, while Escape the Dark Castle represents the small-box sweet spot—practically a party game with fantastic fighting fantasy aesthetics.

The middle tiers span diverse needs: Machina Arcana Third Edition brings dark, pulpy atmosphere and genuine dread; Popper's Ladder delivers light family charm; Space Cadets Away Mission adds sci-fi fun with engaging dice resolution; Fallen Lands captures post-apocalyptic adventure perfectly. Daniel's RPG choice is Folklore with its full expansion suite, offering the deepest character progression despite its quirks. Eldritch Horror brings globe-trotting pulp adventure at its finest, while Tales from the Red Dragon—a recent gem—proves that narrative campaigns can be vibrant, colorful, and genuinely tactical without being overly dark or overly goofy.

----

If you were building your own desert island collection, which game categories would you prioritize, and would your selections match Daniel's thinking?
